items.headline: In a message on the occasion of the anniversary of Israel's independence, Benjamin Netanyahu said that the nuclear program of the Islamic Republic has always been a threat to Israel's existence. items.headline: On Sunday, 30th of April, Gombedkavos "Yunolitsazi" factory suffered a massive fire. In this regard, Golestan Governorate's political and security deputy said that the thick smoke is caused by the burning of "Yonolit" materials and there is nothing to worry about. The images released from the fire site show tall columns of smoke rising to the sky and the scope of the fire

items.headline: The lived experience of Amir Mousavi and the commanders of the armed forces must be narrated. Vice President for Women and Family Affairs, in a meeting with the family of Amir Lieutenant General Seyyed Abdul Rahim Mousavi, while offering his condolences to his family and the people of Iran, and congratulating him on the arrival of Army Day, said: "On this day, Amir Mousavi's vacancy will be filled more than ever."

items.headline: Ismail Baqaei wrote on X channel: "The action of the United States in besieging Iranian ports or coasts is not only a violation of the ceasefire brokered by Pakistan, but also illegal and criminal." The spokesman of the Iranian Foreign Ministry considered this blockade a violation of the United Nations Charter and "Resolution 3314" that "blocking ports or coasts is a drawer."

items.headline: Pope Leo: It is necessary to turn off the weapons and follow the path of dialogue. The leader of the world's Catholic Christians: Pope Leo XIV, at the end of a magnificent mass in the countryside of Luanda, warned about the deterioration of the situation in the war in Ukraine and pointed to the increase in tensions and the expansion of the scope of the battle in Eastern Europe.

items.headline: Citing fallout from the Iran war, IMF cuts the outlook for global growth, expects higher inflation - AP News

Iran Aviation Organization announced the issuance of a license for international flights from Mashhad Airport. According to the announcement of this organization, from tomorrow (Monday, April 31), the international flights of this airport will resume and passengers can buy tickets for foreign trips from or to Mashhad. the organization

items.headline: Donald Trump says that his representatives will arrive in Islamabad tomorrow evening to negotiate with Iran. In a post on his social network Truth Social, the President of the United States warned once again that if Iran does not accept the "fair and reasonable" offer of the United States, all of Iran's power plants and bridges will be destroyed.

items.headline: ­¤ōĪ Exclusive | Iran tightens control over Strait of Hormuz as US breaches commitments: Official ­¤ōī Tehran, IRNA ŌĆō Mahmoud Vaezi, a senior politician and the deputy of the Moderation and Development Party has slammed the United States for breaching promises and agreements, calling
items.fullText: ­¤ōĪ Exclusive | Iran tightens control over Strait of Hormuz as US breaches commitments: Official ­¤ōī Tehran, IRNA ŌĆō Mahmoud Vaezi, a senior politician and the deputy of the Moderation and Development Party has slammed the United States for breaching promises and agreements, calling it a reason for Iran to take complete control of the Strait of Hormuz. In an interview with IRNA on Sunday, Vaezi addressed a number of issues related to the joint US-Israeli war, saying the Islamic Republic had repeatedly warned that any military mischief by the enemies would engulf the entire region. ŌĆŗ He said the US and the Zionist regime had planned to steal Iranian oil resources by attacking the country or simply with their so-called "regime change," but the Iranian people and armed forces gave a crushing response to the aggressors, forcing them to beg for a ceasefire, as was the case during the 12-day imposed war last year. ŌĆŗ The Islamic Republic, in practice, was the victor on the battlefield, and will certainly be the victor in diplomacy as the Iranian negotiators enjoy the comprehensive support of the people, officials, as well as of political and defense institutions. ŌĆŗ Vaezi, who previously served the country as Minister of Communications and Information Technology also gave his assessment of the US naval blockade, saying that the US reneging on its commitments under Iran's proposal caused the armed forces to exercise complete control over the Strait of Hormuz. ŌĆŗ According to him, the Islamic Republic demonstrated its resilience for a long period, and despite the two wars imposed by the United States and the Zionist regime, Iran presented a 10-point plan that paved the way for negotiations in Pakistan. ŌĆŗ Referring to the Islamabad talks that ended without a breakthrough amid excessive and irrational American demands, Vaezi said differences would not be resolved in a single session as the animosity between Iran and the US is deep and 47 years old. ŌĆŗ Hailing the people's presence on the streets, Vaezi noted that the Iranian nation once again showed its commitment to the Islamic Republic and stood up wholeheartedly to defend the country. ŌĆŗ He also commended the administration of President Masoud Pezeshkian for its excellent performance during the war, saying the government did not allow a shortage in the supply of goods and essentials, including fuel, which is worthy of appreciation. ­¤ō▓ @Irna_enŌĆŗ

items.fullText: ­¤¢ź Iraqi president stresses continuation of dialogue to end war in region ­¤ōī Tehran, IRNA ŌĆō The new Iraqi President, Nizar Amidi, exchanged views with Iran's ambassador to Baghdad on ways to strengthen bilateral relations and emphasized the continuation of dialogues to end the war in the region. Speaking in a meeting with Mohammad Kazem Al-e-Sadeq, Amidi emphasized the importance of continuing dialogue, ending crises, and keeping the region away from the dangers of war to strengthen international peace and security. Meanwhile, Al-e-Sadeq congratulated Amidi on his election as President of Iraq and wished him success. ŌĆŗ The two sides exchanged views on bilateral relations and ways to enhance ties in various fields. They also discussed the latest regional developments and the security situation. ­¤ō▓ @Irna_enŌĆŗ

items.headline: An overview of the current ceasefires in the Middle East war, the US and Israel's war with Iran, as well as the war in Lebanon, have been stopped for the time being with two ceasefire agreements. items.fullText: ­¤Æ¼ Iran, Turkiye stress continued political consultations, cooperation ­¤ōī Tehran, IRNA ŌĆō Iran and Turkiye have underscored the need to continue political consultations and expand bilateral cooperation across various fields, following talks between their deputy foreign ministers. Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh met with his Turkish counterpart, Musa Kulaklikaya, on Sunday on the sidelines of the fifth Antalya Diplomacy Forum. ŌĆŗ During the meeting, the two sides reviewed bilateral relations and emphasized the continuation of political consultations and cooperation in different areas. ŌĆŗ Regional issues were also discussed, particularly recent developments resulting from the US-Israeli aggression against Iran. ŌĆŗ The Antalya Diplomacy Forum, held from April 17 to 19, has brought together leaders and senior officials from various countries to discuss global challenges and regional tensions. ŌĆŗ Senior diplomats attending the event are also engaging in talks on pressing international issues and the growing instability affecting different parts of the world. ­¤ō▓ @Irna_enŌĆŗ

items.fullText: ŌÜĪ’ĖÅ US emerges ŌĆśbiggest loserŌĆØ in war on Iran: Spanish Analyst ­¤ōī Tehran, IRNA ŌĆō A Spanish political analyst says the United States has suffered its most significant political, economic, and geopolitical losses in the war on Iran, arguing that Washington has emerged as the primary loser of the conflict. According to Eduardo Meneses, heavy military spending without tangible results, rising global oil prices, and a growing rift with European allies have all contributed to weakening the US position. In a report carried by TeleSUR TV news network, Meneses said Washington has faced clear setbacks in multiple fronts, adding that ŌĆ£to preserve its hegemonic power, the United States appears to have little option left but war.ŌĆØ ŌĆŗ He noted that US military strategy in West Asia is tied to broader efforts to maintain global dominance in the face of rising powers such as China. The ongoing ceasefire, he said, offers Washington a chance to regroup, reassess its strategy, and mitigate the damage sustained during the conflict. ŌĆŗ Meneses stressed that the United States is not genuinely pursuing peace, but rather using the ceasefire as a temporary pause to reorganize, replenish resources, and develop new strategies aimed at weakening Iran. ŌĆŗ Highlighting military shortcomings, he said Washington has failed to achieve its stated objectives, many of which were unrealistic from the outset. Threats by President Donald Trump to ŌĆ£destroy an entire civilizationŌĆØ did not materialize, and efforts to bring about regime change in Iran have also fallen short. ŌĆŗ Contrary to some media narratives, Meneses argued that the Iranian government continues to enjoy broad public support, with society demonstrating a strong and unified response in defense of national sovereignty. ŌĆŗ On the economic front, he described the United States as the biggest loser, pointing to soaring oil prices driven in part by disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. He added that the war has also led to increasing distance between Washington and its European allies, many of whom initially backed the campaign. ŌĆŗ The United States and Israel launched their war of aggression against Iran on February 28, 2026. Iran, acting in self-defense under Article 51 of the UN Charter, has carried out missile and drone attacks targeting Israeli positions and US bases in West Asia. ŌĆŗ The Persian Gulf country has also maintained control over the Strait of Hormuz to ensure the security of the vital waterway through which nearly 20% of the worldŌĆÖs energy supply transmit. ­¤ō▓ @Irna_enŌĆŗ

items.fullText: ŌÜĪ’ĖÅ Brazil voices support for SpainŌĆÖs stance against war in West Asia ­¤ōī Tehran, IRNA ŌĆō Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva has expressed support for Spanish Prime Minister Pedro SanchezŌĆÖs opposition to the conflict in the West Asia region, saying the two nations stand in the same trench in defending peace and diplomacy. He made these remarks during the first SpainŌĆōBrazil bilateral meeting held in Barcelona, according to Latin American satellite news TV TeleSUR. ŌĆŗ Lula said he fully understood SanchezŌĆÖs ŌĆ£no to warŌĆØ position, recalling that he adopted a similar stance in 2003 when Brazil was asked to participate in the Iraq War. He noted that both countries share a common approach to addressing global challenges through solutions that do not rely on extreme or divisive rhetoric. ŌĆŗ Highlighting current international tensions, Lula warned that the world may be entering a new arms race in which civilians, including women and children, are increasingly vulnerable, and technological tools risk replacing human ethical considerations. ŌĆŗ He also emphasized the symbolic significance of meeting in Barcelona, a city that played a historic role during the Spanish Civil War. Lula added that both nations believe democracy must deliver tangible benefits for citizens and said Brazil has much to learn from SpainŌĆÖs experience. ŌĆŗ The Barcelona meeting marks the beginning of three joint international events, including a SpainŌĆōBrazil dialogue, a forum on defending democracy, and a progressive gathering expected to draw thousands of participants. ŌĆŗ Prior to the summit, Lula had cautioned that rising regional tensions could carry serious global economic consequences and urged diplomatic solutions over military confrontation. ŌĆŗ Questioning the motivations behind the escalation, the Spanish prime minister criticized its impact on regional stability, international norms, and humanitarian conditions, including renewed tensions in Iraq and Lebanon and the worsening crisis in the Gaza Strip. ­¤ō▓ @Irna_enŌĆŗ

items.headline: Strait of Hormuz closed again, Iran says, as ships attacked

Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, Speaker of the Islamic Council, announced in a video message on Saturday night, April 29, that the control of the Strait of Hormuz is under the control of the Islamic Republic, and if the actions of the other side continue, maritime traffic in this direction may be restricted. He was referring to a confrontation between the Navy Corps and the Army

He added: "They played a bit of a trick, as they have done for 47 years

items.headline: Iran's Supreme National Security Council announced on Saturday that the commander of Pakistan's army has delivered new American proposals to Iran, but Tehran has not yet responded to them. Meanwhile, US President Donald Trump announced on Friday that Iran has agreed to hand over its enriched uranium reserves.

items.headline: In a report of the attack on the Pasteur Institute in Tehran, Shargh newspaper wrote that sensitive centers and laboratories were not seriously damaged and biological samples were preserved. In this report, it has been emphasized that the investigations about the damage to the Pasteur Institute building show that this scientific center was hit by three waves of rocket attacks on the street.

items.headline: According to the British Maritime Trade Operations Organization, two speedboats of the Revolutionary Guards have targeted an oil tanker in the Strait of Hormuz. According to this organization, the oil tanker and its crew are safe after the incident that happened about 32 kilometers northeast of Oman. Reuters news agency also quoted three sources of the report

items.headline: Following the publication of conflicting news about the opening of the Strait of Hormuz, the central headquarters of Khatam al-Anbia announced on Saturday, April 29, that the strait was again "under the management and strict control of the armed forces". This institution called the reason for this issue "intentionality" of the Americans and called them "banditry and piracy under the new law".

items.headline: In an announcement, the Iranian Civil Aviation Organization announced the reopening of a part of the country's airspace from Saturday, April 29. According to the announcement of the Civil Aviation Organization, a number of airports have been reopened since 7:00 am on Saturday, and the air routes in the eastern part of the Iranian sky are also open for the passage of international flights.

items.headline: Maulvi Abdul Hamid's criticism of extremism, execution and confiscation of property; Emphasis on the necessity of "strong diplomacy" according to Halevash report/ On Friday, 28 April 1405, Maulvi Abdul Hamid, during the Friday prayer ceremony of Zahedan, criticized the extremism in the country's policies, "execution" and "confiscation of the property of opponents", on the need to strengthen diplomacy.

items.headline: No information about the fate of 14 Baloch sailors of a fishing barge after months in the open waters, according to the report of Halesh Vash/Today, Friday 28 April 1405, after nearly four months have passed since the departure of a fishing barge from Kanarak wharf and 48 days since the last contact, there is still no information about the fate of the 14 Baloch crew members of this barge.

items.headline: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first

Trump: The Islamic Republic has agreed to give up nuclear weapons, according to Hale Vash Report / Yesterday, Thursday, April 27, 1405, the President of the United States, Donald Trump, announced that the Islamic Republic of Iran has agreed to not having nuclear weapons and emphasized that there is a "very strong statement" in this regard.

items.headline: Welcoming the ceasefire in Lebanon Ismail Baqaei, the spokesman of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, welcomed the announcement of the ceasefire in Lebanon, and noted that the cessation of war in Lebanon was part of the ceasefire agreement between Iran and the United States with the mediation of Pakistan, and that the Islamic Republic of Iran has been in talks with various regional and international parties since the beginning.

items.headline: The killing of two Baloch youths following the firing by IRGC forces on the border of Kalgan, Golshan city, according to the current report, on Monday, April 24, 1405, at around 7:00 am, two Baloch teenagers who had gone to graze their sheep near Bonser village in the border area of ŌĆŗŌĆŗKalgan, Golshan city, were targeted.
